results.title: JustWrite — Your Writing. No Escape.
results.content:
Just write.

## Honest answers

The things people actually ask before buying.

### Is JustWrite Mac-only?

Yes. JustWrite is a native SwiftUI app built for macOS 14 and later. It's not an Electron app or a web wrapper, and there's no Windows, Linux, or web version.

### Does JustWrite work offline?

Yes. Writing sessions run entirely on your Mac — no connection is needed to start a sprint, and your words never have to leave your machine.

### How do I get my writing out of JustWrite?

When your sprint ends you can copy everything to the clipboard or save it to a file. From there, paste it into iA Writer, Ulysses, Scrivener, Google Docs — wherever you edit.

### How is this different from iA Writer or Ulysses? [...] ### Typewriter Sounds

Authentic mechanical feedback on every keystroke. Special sounds for Enter, Space, and Backspace.

### Native macOS App

Built with SwiftUI for macOS 14+. Not an Electron app. Not a web wrapper. A real Mac app.

results.title: Just Write. | Writing Forums
results.content:
I'm so glad someone started this thread because I was going to do the same, but from a completely different perspective.  
   
 I have to admit I'm getting rather tired of this advice to 'Just write', or more specifically when someone says, "You have to write, even when it's the last thing you feel like doing."  
   
 Why? Why do I have to write? Forcing yourself to write when it's the last thing you feel like doing seems like a pointless exercise to me. It's frustrating, non-productive and disheartening. The words you write during this session will no doubt be scrapped anyway, because your state of mind at the time means you only wrote garbage. [...] > OurJud said:
>
> I'm so glad someone started this thread because I was going to do the same, but from a completely different perspective.  
>    
>  I have to admit I'm getting rather tired of this advice to 'Just write', or more specifically when someone says, "You have to write, even when it's the last thing you feel like doing."  
>    
>  Why? Why do I have to write? Forcing yourself to write when it's the last thing you feel like doing seems like a pointless exercise to me. It's frustrating, non-productive and disheartening. The words you write during this session will no doubt be scrapped anyway, because your state of mind at the time means you only wrote garbage.  
> [...] What's more I think it may actually harm your craving for your story and characters because you'll start to associate it with an unpleasant experience.  
   
 I just don't buy this 'Writing, even bad writing, is good' mentality. It's hokum.  
   
 I haven't even opened my WiP today, because I didn't feel like it. Do I feel frustrated by that fact? Yes. Do I feel like I've wasted another day? Yes. Do I feel like I've failed? Definitely.  
   
 Would I have felt better if I'd forced myself to write? No, I'd have felt worse because it would have been just been further proof that I can't write a novel.

# Unlock your writing creativity. Let ideas flow while writing offline, free from distractions. Do not edit or delete; just keep writing.

## Get the App

### Windows

 Buy   $5.99

### Mac

 Buy   $5.99

### Android / Chrome OS

 [](

### One-time payment

Buy JustWrite once and get lifetime access, including future feature updates and bug fixes.

## Support

What is JustWrite for? [...] ## Support

What is JustWrite for?

JustWrite helps you overcome writer's block through free writing: a technique where you write quickly and continuously, without worrying about structure, grammar, or formatting. Because you cannot edit or delete what you have already written, the app encourages you to keep moving forward and unlock your creative flow.

Why is the app paid?

JustWrite is built by independent developers. Selling the app is what allows us to maintain it and keep developing future versions for more platforms.

Can I try the app before buying?

The app works just like the online version, with one key difference: Focus Mode is available on Windows, Mac and Android, not online.

If the app does not work for you or does not meet your needs, you can request a refund. [...] Do I need a separate license for each device?

For Windows and Mac desktop versions, one license can be used on up to three devices at the same time. The Android version is purchased separately through Google Play and is linked to your Google account under the Play Store's standard terms.

How can I contact you?

Send any questions, suggestions, or issues to hello@calmly.studio
